nodes.label: AI applications include a...
nodes.content: AI applications include advanced web search engines, recommendation systems, understanding human speech, self-driving cars, generative and creative tools, and competing at the highest level in strategic game systems.

nodes.label: AI was founded as an acad...
nodes.content: AI was founded as an academic discipline in 1956 at the Dartmouth Conference by John McCarthy, Marvin Minsky, Nathaniel Rochester, and Claude Shannon.

nodes.label: Deep learning is a subset...
nodes.content: Deep learning is a subset of machine learning based on artificial neural networks with multiple layers. It has transformed image recognition, NLP, and game playing.

nodes.label: The AI winter refers to p...
nodes.content: The AI winter refers to periods of reduced funding and interest in AI research, notably in the 1970s and late 1980s, following over-promising and under-delivering.

nodes.label: Reinforcement learning is...
nodes.content: Reinforcement learning is an area of machine learning where an agent learns to make decisions by taking actions in an environment and receiving rewards or penalties.

nodes.label: AI alignment is the techn...
nodes.content: AI alignment is the technical challenge of ensuring AI systems act in accordance with human intentions and values. It has become a major research focus.

nodes.label: Neural networks are compu...
nodes.content: Neural networks are computing systems inspired by biological neural networks in animal brains. They consist of interconnected nodes (neurons) that process information.

nodes.label: Symbolic AI (GOFAI, Good ...
nodes.content: Symbolic AI (GOFAI, Good Old-Fashioned AI) uses logic and symbols to represent knowledge. Dominant until the 1980s, now complemented by neural approaches.

nodes.label: Expert systems were early...
nodes.content: Expert systems were early AI programs that used knowledge from experts to solve complex problems. Commercial success in the 1980s followed by decline.

nodes.label: Computer vision is a fiel...
nodes.content: Computer vision is a field of AI that trains computers to interpret and understand visual information from images and videos. Applications include facial recognition and autonomous vehicles.

nodes.label: AGI (Artificial General I...
nodes.content: AGI (Artificial General Intelligence) would perform any intellectual task that a human can. Not yet achieved; remains a long-term goal of AI research.

nodes.label: The Turing Test, proposed...
nodes.content: The Turing Test, proposed by Alan Turing in 1950, tests a machine ability to exhibit intelligent behavior equivalent to humans. It remains influential in AI philosophy.

nodes.label: Test: Engagement half-lif...
nodes.content: Test: Engagement half-life tracking. Pursue consciousness-architecture thread for 7 days.

nodes.label: Machine Learning (ML) is ...
nodes.content: Machine Learning (ML) is a field of study in artificial intelligence concerned with the development and study of statistical algorithms that can learn from data and generalize to unseen data.

nodes.label: Artificial Intelligence (...
nodes.content: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is intelligence demonstrated by machines, as opposed to natural intelligence displayed by animals and humans. AI research has been defined as the field of study of intelligent agents.

nodes.label: Token Issue: Conversation...
nodes.content: Token Issue: Conversation history was 54k+ tokens, unbounded growth

nodes.label: Large Language Models (LL...
nodes.content: Large Language Models (LLMs) like GPT are trained on massive text corpora to generate human-like text. They emerged as a dominant AI paradigm in the 2020s.

nodes.label: Natural Language Processi...
nodes.content: Natural Language Processing (NLP) enables computers to understand, interpret, and generate human language. Used in chatbots, translation, and sentiment analysis.
